core_mechanism: extracting a per-step degradation rate by composing randomly-drawn operations into sequences of increasing length and fitting the exponential decay of a survival/overlap signal versus sequence length, with the fitted offset absorbing fixed endpoint (preparation/measurement) errors so only the per-operation rate is estimated
brief_reason: The abstract estimates a computationally relevant per-gate error from long sequences of randomly chosen gates in a way that does not rely on accurate state preparation and measurement, which is exactly isolating a per-step decay rate from endpoint offsets via random-sequence composition.
